name: South Korea
summary: Day 76 of Hormuz blockade. **A13 HMM NAMU SITUATION UNCHANGED — South Korean attribution position remains 'various actors' (FM Cho Hyun May 13 hedge carries forward). No fresh forensics; South Korean Presidential Office continues to condemn the strike; opposition People Power Party continues to publicly criticise the government for omitting Iran. No new amplification from US/Israeli press today — Seoul institutionally maintaining diplomatic-finesse posture.** **C4 KOSPI 7,941.95 (+1.2% 1D, +36.7% 1M, +39.9% 3M)** — REBOUND CONTINUES into the Trump-Xi summit narrative; AI super-cycle (Samsung+SK Hynix 42% of KOSPI; chip exports +150% YoY first 10 days May; JPM raised KOSPI 12-month bull-case target to 10,000; Goldman 9,000 12m target). USD/KRW ₩1,489.81 (-0.2% 1D) = currency steadying as KOSPI rally absorbs FX outflow. **Helium 2-week-clock (TrendForce March 24) at Day 73 since Ras Laffan halt March 2 = TIME-SENSITIVE EXHAUSTION TRIGGER MOST LIKELY TO ACTIVATE WITHIN MAY 16-23 WINDOW**. SK hynix continues 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED, one-sided); no Samsung/SK hynix production-halt announcement. **C4 cross-contamination risk**: 30% of global helium routed through Qatar Ras Laffan; if helium clock expires (mid-May → now), KOSPI is the most exposed major index. **Per markets dossier H: KOSPI/SMH/Taiwan are the most asymmetric DOWN trades if helium-shutdown materialises — pricing zero supply-chain risk while supply-chain risk is the most quantifiable real-world risk in the entire complex.** Phil Kornbluth's 'tsunami still a thousand miles offshore' held one more session. PRIOR (May 13): **A13 SOUTH KOREAN GOVERNMENT FORMALLY CONFIRMED HMM NAMU MAY 4 INCIDENT WAS EXTERNAL STRIKE — investigation found TWO UNIDENTIFIED AIRBORNE OBJECTS struck stern at approximately one-minute intervals ('double-tap' drone pattern; first near rudder, second engine room damage); MINE + TORPEDO formally ruled out (May 10-11). FM CHO HYUN (May 13 Korea Herald): 'TOO EARLY TO DETERMINE WHO WAS RESPONSIBLE,' explicitly noting 'various actors, including militias, could have been involved.' OPPOSITION PEOPLE POWER PARTY publicly criticised government for omitting Iran from initial findings — domestic political pressure to name Tehran building, but Seoul institutionally maintaining 'various actors' hedge.** **C4 KOSPI 7,855.47 +2.8% 1D = REBOUND after May 12 -1.7%; FM Cho 'too early' + PPP opposition pressure DEFUSE immediate 'Korea forced to name Iran' diplomatic spiral. The double-tap drone forensic confirmation is OPERATIONAL fact; the diplomatic implication is HEDGED. The KOSPI -1.7% May 12 selloff in yesterday's brief is now BETTER-UNDERSTOOD AS PARTIAL REPRICING OF DIPLOMATIC-FRICTION RISK rather than definitive Iran-attribution event.** USD/KRW ₩1,489.12 +1.0% 1D = DISCONNECT (currency says capital outflow, equity says relief). **Korean helium-fab buffer ~4 WEEKS REMAINING (Kornbluth 'tsunami a thousand miles offshore' thesis intact one more session); cycle's helium-tail trade thesis NOT challenged by new data this session.** SK hynix continues 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED, one-sided). NVIDIA earnings May 28 next major catalyst for AI semis bid. ORIGINAL CONTEXT (May 12): **KOSPI 7,690.59 May 12 (-1.7% 1D, +33.1% 1M, +39.3% 3M) — FIRST PROFIT-TAKING SESSION AFTER EXTRAORDINARY VERTICAL RUN.** USD/KRW ₩1,488.28 (+1.9% 1D) — won materially weakening on (i) HMM Namu attack on Korea-flagged vessel + (ii) KOSPI capital outflow. **B9 HMM NAMU FORENSICS PUBLIC MAY 11: Korean joint investigation team confirmed vessel struck May 4 anchored off UAE was hit by 2 unidentified aerial objects ~1 min apart at the stern, causing engine-room fire; MINE AND TORPEDO RULED OUT by damage pattern; engine debris recovered for forensic analysis. SEOUL STOPPED SHORT OF PUBLICLY BLAMING IRAN** — creating diplomatic friction with Trump, who had publicly attributed to Iran from the start. First commercial-vessel strike of cycle with conclusive public forensic investigation. Korea Times: 'may shift Seoul stance' on potential South Korean involvement in any Hormuz protection mission. **Korea has gone from 'passive helium victim' to 'active diplomatic actor' with HMM Namu forensics.** Won weakness suggests foreign capital trimming Korea exposure on diplomatic-friction risk while Korea-domestic capital absorbs at lower prices. KOSPI -1.7% on a 7,800-printing market is shallow — real test is whether 7,500 holds within 5 sessions. **Helium tail-risk timeline tightening: Korean fab buffer now ~4 WEEKS REMAINING to mid-June exhaustion.** Tier-1 chipmaker production-halt falsification still HOLDS but window contracting. SK hynix continues 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED — one-sided); TrendForce 'two-week clock' (March 24) now ~7 weeks elapsed without confirmed halt. SMH $576.31 (+1.7% 1D, +31.9% 1M); AI infrastructure CapEx demand from Microsoft/Meta/Amazon unbroken. **STRUCTURAL ASSESSMENT (still most reversible position in world if helium buffers deplete past mid-June):** Crisis-linked share rising from ~5% (helium tail) to ~40% (helium + HMM Namu + diplomatic friction).
